transcript.text: Let me give you some tips and tricks on how to become a confident speaker and
there some body parts can help you. First of all there's your eyes. Try to
make eye contact with your audience. If you're looking up or towards the wall
or even worse down, people are going to get the impression that you're not
really confident, that you're looking for your words. So dare to look at
your audience. Secondly, there's your smile. Put on a friendly smile. I'm not
talking about a toothpaste commercial like this, but just a friendly face.
People will have the impression that you're absolutely not nervous when
you're smiling. You come across confident. Furthermore, research has
shown that people who are friendly, their message will be believed easier
and will be remembered longer. So eye contact and a smile. And then there's a
third element and that's your hands. Literally reach out to your audience
to make the physical distance between yourself and the audience shorter. So
don't put your hands behind your back or in your pockets because then it
might seem as if you've got something to hide. You've got a message that
you want to share with the audience and you can't share anything with your
hands behind your back. Now if you use your hands, make sure that your
movements are big enough, slow enough and deliberate enough. So no flapping
